picks.rationale: The Orlando Magic are at home for a crucial Game 3 with the series tied 1-1, providing a significant home-court advantage. While Jonathan Isaac is doubtful for Orlando, the Pistons are coming off a dominant win, which can sometimes lead to a slight dip in focus. The Magic have won three of their last five head-to-head matchups against the Pistons, demonstrating their ability to compete and win in this series. Orlando's solid defense and their success in forcing Detroit turnovers in previous games should help them keep this game close and cover the spread.

The Oklahoma City Thunder will be without key offensive player Jalen Williams (hamstring), a significant loss that will impact their scoring and playmaking. The Phoenix Suns are at home, down 0-2 in the series, and will be playing with desperation to avoid a commanding 3-0 deficit. Despite their own injury concerns with Grayson Allen and Jordan Goodwin listed as questionable/doubtful, the Suns have shown they can cover large spreads against the Thunder, doing so in two of their last five head-to-head losses. Given the Thunder's key injury and the Suns' home desperation, covering a +9.5 spread is a strong possibility.

The New York Knicks are in a must-win situation, trailing the series 1-2 after losing two incredibly close games. Teams facing a potential 3-1 deficit often exhibit heightened urgency and focus, which should benefit the Knicks. The betting line has seen movement from Knicks -1.5 to -2.5, indicating that sharp money is favoring New York to bounce back strongly. The Knicks' physical style of play tends to travel well, and they will be focused on controlling the tempo and limiting turnovers to even the series.

Two of the first three games in this playoff series have finished well below the total line, establishing a strong trend for lower-scoring contests. The Minnesota Timberwolves have showcased dominant defense, holding the Nuggets to just 96 points in Game 3 and effectively limiting Nikola Jokic's efficiency. The Nuggets have struggled offensively, particularly from beyond the arc, shooting only 30% from three-point range in the series. With Aaron Gordon questionable and Peyton Watson out for Denver, their offensive depth and defensive versatility are compromised, further supporting an Under outcome.

With Jalen Williams confirmed OUT due to a hamstring injury, Chet Holmgren is expected to take on a significantly increased scoring role as the Thunder's clear second option. Holmgren averages 17.1 points per game this season and has historically performed well against the Suns, averaging 18.8 points in six head-to-head matchups, exceeding this line four times. The minimal adjustment to his scoring total despite Williams' absence presents a valuable opportunity for the Over, as his usage will undoubtedly rise.

The Carolina Hurricanes have been dominant in this series, holding a commanding 3-0 lead and allowing only three goals across three playoff games. They enter Game 4 with strong recent form, having won seven of their last eight games overall and five of their last six against Ottawa. Ottawa's defense is further weakened by the potential absence of Jake Sanderson, who left Game 3 with an apparent arm injury. The market has reacted to Carolina's strong performance, with public money heavily backing the Hurricanes moneyline, reflecting confidence in their ability to complete the sweep.

This series has been characterized by low-scoring affairs, with the Hurricanes allowing just three goals across three playoff games. Both teams exhibit a strong trend towards the under, with Carolina's last six games and Ottawa's six of their last seven going under the total. The betting market has also seen the total line move down from 5.5 -130 on the over to 5.5 -104 on the under, indicating sharp money favors a low-event game. This aligns with the tight, defensive style often seen in playoff hockey, especially in elimination games.

The Minnesota Wild are playing at home, where they have a favorable 3-1 record in their last four meetings against the Dallas Stars. Dallas is significantly impacted by injuries, with key offensive players Roope Hintz and Tyler Seguin both out, and Esa Lindell and Jake Oettinger questionable. The Wild are facing a crucial bounce-back spot after losing Game 3 in double overtime and are motivated to avoid a 3-1 series deficit. Public money is heavily on the Wild moneyline, indicating strong belief in their ability to tie the series at home.

picks.pick: Jason Robertson Under 3.5 Shots on Goal
picks.rationale: Action Network's PRO Projections have identified a significant 11.2% edge on Jason Robertson going Under 3.5 Shots on Goal for this game. While Robertson is a volume shooter, his recent 76% shot-on-goal rate is unsustainable over a longer period. Minnesota's defensive adjustments and a return to a standard regulation pace after a wide-open Game 3 are expected to limit his opportunities. Jason Robertson is an active player for the Dallas Stars, making this a valid prop bet.

The Philadelphia Flyers hold a commanding 3-0 series lead and have won seven of their last ten head-to-head matchups against the Penguins. Their recent form is excellent, with a 9-1-0 record in their last 10 games and a strong goal differential of +20. Despite starting goalie Dan Vladar being a game-time decision due to an arm injury, he finished Game 3, and the Flyers have demonstrated strong team play throughout the series. Playing at home with a chance to complete a sweep provides significant motivation for Philadelphia.

The Baltimore Orioles are in a favorable position against a struggling Garrett Crochet, who carries a 7.88 ERA and a 1.87 HR/9 rate this season. Baltimore's offense has been performing well, ranking in the top seven scoring teams over the past week and consistently scoring at least seven runs in their recent victories. The Red Sox enter this game having lost four consecutive games and seven of their last ten. Additionally, public betting trends show significant confidence in the Orioles, particularly on the runline, suggesting sharp money is backing Baltimore.

Seattle's starting pitcher, Bryan Woo, is projected to be a 'Cy Young candidate' with an impressive 2.25 ERA, 2.48 xERA, and a 2.27 FIP, holding opponents to a .204 batting average. The Mariners secured a 3-2 victory over the Cardinals in the opening game of this series yesterday. Despite a 4-6 record in their last ten games, Seattle is currently on a two-game winning streak. This strong pitching matchup and recent momentum give the Mariners a significant edge in this contest.

The New York Yankees are on an impressive eight-game winning streak and convincingly defeated the Astros 12-4 in the first game of this series. Yankees' projected starter Ryan Weathers is coming off a dominant performance, having pitched 7.1 scoreless innings with eight strikeouts in his last outing. In contrast, Houston's projected starter, Mike Burrows, has a concerning 1.69 home runs allowed per nine innings this season. The Yankees' strong form, pitching advantage, and recent offensive explosion make them a confident pick to cover the spread.

picks.pick: Aaron Judge to hit a Home Run
picks.rationale: Aaron Judge is active and confirmed in the Yankees' lineup for today's game. He possesses elite batted ball metrics, including a 24.2% barrel rate, and has already hit nine home runs this season. The game is being played at Minute Maid Park, which is known for its 'Crawford Boxes' that favor right-handed power hitters. Facing Astros pitcher Mike Burrows, who has a high home run per nine innings rate of 1.69, further increases Judge's probability of hitting a home run.

The head-to-head history between the Miami Marlins and San Francisco Giants strongly favors Miami, with the Marlins winning four of their last five overall matchups. More specifically, the Giants have lost all five of their most recent home games when facing the Marlins. This consistent historical dominance, particularly at Oracle Park, indicates a significant advantage for Miami in this contest. The Marlins also enter this game with a decent 3-2 record over their last five outings.

picks.game: Red Bull New York @ FC Cincinnati
picks.rationale: FC Cincinnati is severely hampered by an extensive injury list, particularly in defense, with key players like Matt Miazga, Obinna Nwobodo, Miles Robinson, and Nick Hagglund all reported out. This defensive vulnerability has directly contributed to recent high-scoring matches, including a 4-4 draw against NYCFC and a 3-3 draw against Chicago Fire. Red Bull New York also recently defeated FC Cincinnati 4-2, demonstrating their ability to exploit these defensive weaknesses. Given Cincinnati's depleted backline and both teams' recent propensity for goals, the Over 3.5 total is a high-confidence play.

Vancouver Whitecaps are in exceptional form, leading the MLS with a 7-0-1 record and an impressive +18 goal difference, including a strong 6-1-0 home record. Conversely, the Colorado Rapids are struggling with a 4-1-4 record and a leaky defense that has conceded 15 goals this season. The Rapids are also significantly impacted by injuries and a suspension to key players such as Josh Atencio, Ted Ku DiPietro, and Connor Ronan. The Whitecaps have a strong head-to-head record against the Rapids, winning 4 of their last 5 encounters. All these factors point to a dominant home victory for Vancouver.

picks.game: San Jose Earthquakes @ St. Louis CITY SC
picks.rationale: The San Jose Earthquakes enter this match in outstanding form, boasting an impressive 8-0-1 record this season. In stark contrast, St. Louis CITY SC has struggled significantly, holding a poor 1-3-4 record. Despite San Jose's clear superiority in current performance, the moneyline for this match is surprisingly even at +150 for both teams. This line suggests the market may be undervaluing San Jose's consistent winning form and overestimating St. Louis's home advantage, creating a strong value opportunity for the Earthquakes to secure another victory.

Both Orlando City SC and D.C. United are dealing with extensive injury lists, which often leads to defensive instability. Orlando City has a significant -16 goal differential, having scored 10 goals while conceding 26 in just nine games, averaging 2.9 goals conceded per match. D.C. United also concedes an average of 1.4 goals per game. Orlando's offense has shown recent improvement, scoring six goals in their last four matches, including a 4-1 victory in their most recent outing. Given the defensive vulnerabilities on both sides and Orlando's recent offensive uptick, this match is poised for more than 2.5 goals.

Rodolfo Vieira, an accomplished BJJ World Champion, boasts an 11-4-0 record with a remarkable nine of his eleven wins coming by submission, including five finishes in his six UFC victories. He has maintained solid recent form, winning three of his last five fights. His opponent, Eric McConico, lacks detailed recent form information, suggesting Vieira's elite grappling and proven finishing prowess will be a significant advantage. Vieira's submission-heavy style and experience make him a high-confidence pick against an opponent with an unestablished UFC track record.

Youssef Zalal enters this main event bout on an impressive eight-fight win streak, demonstrating significant momentum and confidence. He is currently ranked #7 in the featherweight division and is making his first UFC main event appearance, indicating high motivation. While Aljamain Sterling is a former UFC Bantamweight Champion and ranked #5, his transition to featherweight means his form in this division is less established. Zalal's current streak, ranking, and main event debut provide a strong edge for this pick.

Victor Valenzuela holds a 13-4-0 professional record and is coming off a TKO victory just 30 days ago, showcasing active and successful recent performance. He previously had a six-fight win streak before his last loss, highlighting his capability for sustained success. With seven KOs and six first-round finishes, Valenzuela possesses significant striking power and a strong finishing instinct. Max Griffin, while experienced, has a more inconsistent recent record of LWLWL in his last five fights, giving Valenzuela the edge in current form and finishing ability.
